LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JONATHAN FRIED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT, PLAINTIFF'S PERFORMANCE, DEFENDANT'S BREACH, AND DAMAGES. THERE IS NOTHING WRONG TO REFERRING TO THE EDD PROCEEDINGS AND DEFENDANT CANNOT DEMURRER TO AN ALLEGATION. THE RETALIATION CAUSES OF ACTION SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE PROTECTED ACTIVITY. PARAGRAPH 7 SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES PAGA EXHAUSTION. EVEN IF THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE 7/5/12 LETTER, IT DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE A FAILURE TO EXHAUST. LABOR 2699.3(B)(2)(A)(IV) PROVIDES: "THE SUPERIOR COURT SHALL NOT DISMISS THE ACTION FOR NONMATERIAL DIFFERENCES IN FACTS OR THEORIES BETWEEN THOSE CONTAINED IN THE NOTICE OF VIOLATION PROVIDED TO THE DIVISION AND EMPLOYER PURSUANT TO PARAGRAPH (1) AND THE COMPLAINT FILED WITH THE COURT." THE CITATION TO VILLACRES V. ABM INDUSTRIES INC. (2010) 189 CAL.APP.4TH 562, IS OFF POINT. THAT WAS NOT A PLEADING CASE AND A PARTY CANNOT DEMURRER TO A CLAIM FOR RELIEF. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS SHOWING THAT THE MISCLASSIFICATION WAS WILLFUL. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH CRC 2.112(4). GIVEN THAT DEFECT, IT IS UNNECESSARY TO RULE SPECIFICALLY ON THE VARIOUS OTHER GROUNDS. HOWEVER, ANY AMENDED COMPLAINT SHOULD MORE CLEARLY STATE FACTS SUPPORTING A LEGAL BASIS FOR THE RELIEF SOUGHT AGAINST THE DEFENDANTS TO WHOM IT IS ADDRESSED. NEITHER A STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS NOR A FORM OF RELIEF IS ITSELF A BASIS FOR A CAUSE OF ACTION. FRAUD-BASED CLAIMS MUST BE STATED WITH PARTICULARITY AGAINST EACH DEFENDANT TO WHOM THEY ARE ADDRESSED. TETI, CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST EITHER COMPLY WITH THE PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS OF SECTION 1714.10 OR PLEAD NON-CONCLUSORY FACTS THAT WOULD BASE A CLAIM AGAINST THEM ON SOMETHING OTHER THAN THEIR CONDUCT IN THE COURSE OF THEIR REPRESENTATION OF MS. THE COURT TREATS THIS MOTION TO DISMISS AS A DEMURRER PER BARRAGAN V. BANCO BCH (1986) 188 CAL.APP.3D 283, 299. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. Off Calendar Mar-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dustin L. Collier, of Collier Law Firm, Tel. 510.250.9606, for plaintiffs;
Drew F. Teti, of Law Offices of Drew F. Teti, Tel. 415.813.1177, for plaintiffs;
Raymond T. Burke, of Law Offices of Michael Heath, Tel. 415.931.4207, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JONATHAN FRIED Motion To Vacate Certain Portions Of The Courts Order Dated January 27 2017, Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 1008 is denied. Mr. Fried's motion is not based on any new law, facts or circumstances and thus is not a proper CCP 1008 motion. Even if the Court were to treat the motion as an invitation for him to exercise his discretion to reconsider the January 27, 2017, order per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094, the Court would not reach a different decision. By this motion, Mr. Fried, whose willful inactions led to the entry of the judgment, asks to be relieved of the consequences of those inactions and not to be held accountable for the costs those inactions imposed on plaintiff Karma Quick and her counsel. Granting the motion is thus not only prejudicial to Ms. Quick, but also unfair and inequitable. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. CROSS DEFENDANTS KARMA QUICK, DUSTIN COLLIER and DREW TETI'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is granted. Cross-Request for Second Notice is granted. The first and second causes of action in the Second Amended Cross-Complaint ("SACC") arise out of an act in furtherance of Cross-Defendants' right to petition. The SACC alleges that Cross-Defendant Karma Quick provided privileged documents and information to her attorneys, Cross-Defendants Dustin L. Collier and Drew F. Teti, in order for them to prepare for a wage and hour suit against Cross-Plaintiff. (SACC, pars. 44 and 45.) The privileged documents were "reasonably necessary to the preparation and prosecution of the action." (Fox Searchlight Pictures, Inc. v. Paladino (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 294, 314-15.) This is evidenced by the fact that the documents were responsive to a discovery request propounded by Cross-Plaintiff himself. (Declaration of Dustin L. Collier, par. 4.) Although the SACC alleges that Cross-Defendants also provided privileged documents to third-parties outside of the litigation context, "[t]he pleading of other' indisputably, unprotected theories of liability does not eliminate or reduce the chilling effect on the exercise of free speech and petition." (Haight Ashbury Free Clinics, Inc. v. Happening House Ventures (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539, 1551.) Thus, Cross-Defendants have met their burden of making a threshold showing that the first and second causes of action arise from protected activity. Cross-Plaintiff has not met his burden of showing a probability of prevailing on the causes of action. Cross-Plaintiff has not submitted any evidence in support of his opposition. Order signed. Judge: Richard A. Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANTS KARMA QUICK, DUSTIN COLLIER & DREW TETI'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The First Amended Cross-Complaint fails to comply with CRC 2.112(4). Given that defect, it is unnecessary to rule specifically on the various other grounds. However, any amended complaint should more clearly state facts supporting a legal basis for the relief sought against the defendants to whom it is addressed. Neither a statute of limitations nor a form of relief is itself a basis for a cause of action. Fraud-based claims must be stated with particularity against each defendant to whom they are addressed. If claims are to be asserted against either Mr. Collier or Mr. Teti, cross-complainant must either comply with the procedural requirements of section 1714.10 or plead non-conclusory facts that would base a claim against them on something other than their conduct in the course of their representation of Ms. Quick in connection with this dispute. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT JONATHAN FRIED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. The amended complaint sufficiently pleads the terms of the contract, plaintiff's performance, defendant's breach, and damages. There is nothing wrong to referring to the EDD proceedings and defendant cannot demurrer to an allegation. The retaliation causes of action sufficiently allege protected activity. Paragraph 7 sufficiently alleges PAGA exhaustion. Even if the Court takes judicial notice of the 7/5/12 letter, it does not demonstrate a failure to exhaust. Labor 2699.3(b)(2)(A)(iv) provides: "The superior court shall not dismiss the action for nonmaterial differences in facts or theories between those contained in the notice of violation provided to the division and employer pursuant to paragraph (1) and the complaint filed with the court." The citation to Villacres v. ABM Industries Inc. (2010) 189 Cal.App.4th 562, is off point. That was not a pleading case and a party cannot demurrer to a claim for relief. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges facts showing that the misclassification was willful. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported.

Ntc Of Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Dec-21-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dustin Collier, Esq. and Drew Teti, Esq., 510.610.0339, for plaintiff Karma Quick.
Jonathan Fried, Esq., 415.986.7000, defendant in pro per.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANT JONATHAN FRIED'S Motion To Dismiss is denied. The Court treats this motion to dismiss as a demurrer per Barragan v. Banco BCH (1986) 188 Cal.App.3d 283, 299. The demurrer is overruled. The face of the complaint fails to demonstrate that plaintiff failed to exhaust an applicable administrative remedy. Indeed, the allegations at par. 7, which the Court takes as true, indicate otherwise. Plaintiff's request for sanctions/fees under CCP 128.5 is denied. That statute does not apply to this action because this action was commenced after December 31, 1994. See CCP 128.5(b)(1). Defendant has until January 21, 2013 to file responsive pleading.
Additional orders: the Court denies plaintiff's motion to strike defendant's non statutory motion to dismiss. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976
